# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline with SBOM & Security Scanning in GitHub Actions (v2)
on: [push] # Set this event when a new push happens on your repository, or schedule it to run at set intervals.  
jobs: 
  build-and-deploy:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest      
        
    steps:         
      - name: Checkout code       
        uses: actions/checkout@v2 # Set up our repository checking out in the GitHub Action environment.  
          
      - name: Build and test with node     # This step builds your application using Node (npm).  You can customize it as per requirement for different languages or frameworks        
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2       // Set up our CI runner's runtime    Environment variables set in the GitHub Action environment are available to all steps Run `echo ${{ runner.os }}` inside step     # Checking out code, running npm install and building  Use container here for consistent build process        
        with:           name=v14   // Set Node version (default is latest stable release)       Install any dependencies installed in your project using package-lock      Run `npm test --if-present` to run tests if present, otherwise skip them.    # Cache node modules after setup     Yarn cache can be reused between builds: npm ci   // Caching yarn packages       
        ~/.yarn/cache       /// Reusing cached dependencies instead of downloading and reinstalling each time  .bin      /user_home          Run `npm audit --auditable` to identify any known vulnerable package    # Outputs the results in a JSON format     ${{ steps.list-results.outputs.all }}        
        env:            name=v14   // Set Node version (default is latest stable release)  -name "${npm_package_}" npm test --if present, otherwise skip them          .bin       /user_home           Run `npx audit` to identify any known vulnerable package      Yarn cache can be reused between builds: ${{ runner.cache_dir }}/yarn-v1/lib       
        ${npm_package_.name}// Caching yarn packages    # Running the build command     node -e "${"exports"}: require('./build')(module, exports)" -- /app  .env      export GITHUB_TOKEN=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}   // Set GitHub token for access to certain actions in workflows (secrets)       
          - name: Build and Deploy     # This step builds your application using Node, then deploys it on the server  Install `firebase-tools` package with npm or Yarn    Run following commands inside CI/CD pipeline         Use container here for consistent build process   // Set up firestore security rules      
          - run: yarn && node deploy.js      # This step is to automatically publish changes in FireBase Hooks and Sentry     Adding secret gitHubToken=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  NPM_TOKEN=\$NPM_TOKEN firebase-functions@ Functions should now run locally using `npx firebase emulators:start --only functions` Firestore security rules (FireBase) are being written automatically for you.      # To ensure that CI/CD pipeline doesn'
